response.justification: The candidate brings roughly 17 years of broad engineering experience and genuine entrepreneurial initiative (GISCollective founder, open-source contributions, public workshops), which partially satisfy the seniority floor. However, the role is a director-level AI strategy IC requiring recognized, demonstrated AI expertise and the ability to advise strategic enterprise customers on cloud and AI adoption. The candidate's AI work is entirely recent (April 2025 onward) and scoped to infrastructure tooling (MCP generators, agentic scaffolding) rather than building or shipping AI-powered products or platforms at scale. There is no evidence of cloud architecture ownership, customer-facing strategic advisory experience, or the kind of external AI thought-leadership the role demands.
strengths: 17+ years of hands-on engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and distributed systems, clearly meeting the experience floor
Active founder of an open-source geo-data platform (GISCollective) demonstrates entrepreneurial initiative and end-to-end product ownership
Current engagement with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling (re:cinq) shows a real pivot toward agentic AI development work
concerns: AI expertise is shallow and very recent (~1 year), with no demonstrated history of building or shipping AI-powered products or platforms at meaningful scale
No evidence of cloud architecture strategy or advisory work at enterprise scale; AWS CDK usage in a pricing team does not constitute cloud platform expertise
No signals of director-level IC scope: no strategic customer advising, no cross-organization technical leadership, no external AI thought-leadership beyond niche workshops

key_factors.factor: Breadth of engineering experience (17+ years, multi-domain)
Absence of strategic advisory and director-level IC evidence
